name: agentsfleet
description: Fleet Delivery Control Plane — long-lived, event-driven autonomous workers (Fleets) scoped to a workspace.

operations.description: Provision a new Fleet in the given workspace. Returns the fleet_id used for subsequent operations.

Partial update of a Fleet. Body fields are optional and presence-based: config_json (replace config) and/or status ("active" to resume, "stopped" to halt, "killed" to mark terminal). The 'paused' state is platform-only — anomaly-gate auto-halt — and rejected here.

Hard-delete a Fleet and cascade its history (events, telemetry, sessions, approval gates, memory entries). Precondition: status='killed' — kill the fleet before deleting. Returns 204 on success, 409 if not yet killed.
Post a chat message to a running Fleet's loop.
List the Fleet's event history with pagination.
Server-Sent Events stream of new events as they arrive.
Read the caller tenant's credit-pool balance, exhaustion state, and free-trial window.
List newest-first credit-pool charge telemetry rows for the caller tenant.
Read the per-renewal metering slices behind one tenant charge.

Deliver an inbound event to a Fleet. Body must be Hash-Based Message Authentication Code with Secure Hash Algorithm 256 (HMAC-SHA256) signed with the workspace credential matching the Fleet's trigger.source (e.g. vault row fleet:github, field webhook_secret); the provider-specific signature header is verified upstream. Idempotent within 24h via event_id.

revenue.description: Users may supply their own Large Language Model (LLM) API keys. agentsfleet meters hosted execution against a credit pool: event receipts are free, active runtime is $0.0001/sec under both postures, and platform-managed posture also adds provider token costs. New tenants receive a $5 starter credit. Never stores or marks up LLM provider costs.
